nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=The OpenSSL version on the remote host has reached the end of life (EOL) and should not be used anymore.|insight=|affected=|impact=An EOL version of OpenSSL is not receiving any security updates from the vendor. nvt.solution: Update the OpenSSL version on the remote host to a still supported version.

result.name: PostgreSQL Multiple Vulnerabilities (Aug 2025) - Windows

nvt.name: PostgreSQL Multiple Vulnerabilities (Aug 2025) - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=PostgreSQL is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following flaws exist:
- CVE-2025-8713: Optimizer statistics can expose sampled data within a view, partition, or child table
- CVE-2025-8714: pg_dump lets superuser of origin server execute arbitrary code in psql client
- CVE-2025-8715: pg_dump newline in object name executes arbitrary code in psql client and in restore target server|affected=PostgreSQL prior to version 13.22, 14.x prior to 14.19, 15.x prior to 15.14, 16.x prior to 16.10 and 17.x prior to 17.6.|impact=|solution=Update to version 13.22, 14.19, 15.14, 16.10, 17.6 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 15.12 Fixed version: 15.14 Installation path / port: C:\Program Files\PostgreSQL\15

result.name: Node.js < 18.20.2, 19.x < 20.12.2, 21.x < 21.7.3 Command Injection Vulnerability (BatBadBut) - Windows

nvt.name: Node.js < 18.20.2, 19.x < 20.12.2, 21.x < 21.7.3 Command Injection Vulnerability (BatBadBut) - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Node.js is prone to a command injection vulnerability on Windows dubbed 'BatBadBut'.|insight=Due to the improper handling of batch files in child_process.spawn / child_process.spawnSync, a malicious command line argument can inject arbitrary commands and achieve code execution even if the shell option is not enabled.|affected=Node.js versions prior to 18.20.2, starting from 19.x and prior to 20.12.2 and 21.x prior to 21.7.3 on Windows.
Vendor note: It's important to note that End-of-Life versions are always affected when a security release occurs.|impact=|solution=Update to version 18.20.2, 20.12.2, 21.7.3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 18.14.2 Fixed version: 18.20.2 Installation path / port: unknown

result.name: Node.js 18.x < 18.18.2, 20.x < 20.8.1 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Windows

nvt.name: Node.js 18.x < 18.18.2, 20.x < 20.8.1 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Node.js is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2023-45143: Cookie headers are not cleared in cross-domain redirect in undici-fetch
- CVE-2023-44487: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset
- CVE-2023-39331: Permission model improperly protects against path traversal
- CVE-2023-39332: Path traversal through path stored in Uint8Array
- CVE-2023-38552: Integrity checks according to policies can be circumvented
- CVE-2023-39333: Code injection via WebAssembly export names|affected=Node.js versions 18.x and 20.x.
- CVE-2023-44487: This vulnerability affects all users of HTTP/2 servers in all active release lines 18.x and 20.x
- CVE-2023-39331, CVE-2023-39332: This vulnerability affects all users using the experimental permission model in Node.js 20.x
- CVE-2023-38552: This vulnerability affects all users using the experimental policy mechanism in all active release lines: 18.x and, 20.x
- CVE-2023-39333: This vulnerability affects users of the --experimental-wasm-modules command line option in all active release lines 18.x and 20.x|impact=|solution=Update to version 18.18.2, 20.8.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 18.14.2 Fixed version: 18.18.2 Installation path / port: unknown

result.name: Microsoft Windows Unquoted Path Vulnerability (SMB Login)

nvt.name: Microsoft Windows Unquoted Path Vulnerability (SMB Login)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=The script tries to detect Windows 'Uninstall' registry entries and 'Services' using an unquoted path containing at least one whitespace.|insight=If the path contains spaces and is not surrounded by quotation marks, the Windows API has to guess where to find the referenced program. If e.g. a service is using the following unquoted path:
C:\Program Files\Folder\service.exe
then a start of the service would first try to run:
C:\Program.exe
and if not found:
C:\Program Files\Folder\service.exe
afterwards. In this example the behavior allows a local attacker with low privileges and write permissions on C:\ to place a malicious Program.exe which is then executed on a service/host restart or during the uninstallation of a software.
NOTE: Currently only 'Services' using an unquoted path are reported as a vulnerability. The 'Uninstall' vulnerability requires an Administrator / User to actively uninstall the affected software to trigger this vulnerability.|affected=Software installing an 'Uninstall' registry entry or 'Service' on Microsoft Windows using an unquoted path containing at least one whitespace.|impact=A local attacker could gain elevated privileges by inserting an executable file in the path of the affected service or uninstall entry.|solution=Either put the listed vulnerable paths in quotation by manually using the onboard Registry editor or contact your vendor to get an update for the specified software that fixes this vulnerability.|vuldetect=|solution_type=Workaround
nvt.solution: Either put the listed vulnerable paths in quotation by manually using the onboard Registry editor or contact your vendor to get an update for the specified software that fixes this vulnerability.

result.description:
The following services are using an 'unquoted' service path:
DisplayName|Name|PathName AicWifiService|AicWifiService|C:\Program Files\Tenda\AicWifiService.exe Wondershare Native Push Service|NativePushService|C:\Users\Danny\AppData\Local\Wondershare\Wondershare NativePush\WsNativePushService.exe

result.name: Python Multiple Vulnerabilities (Aug 2024) - Windows

nvt.name: Python Multiple Vulnerabilities (Aug 2024) - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Python is prone to an infinite loop vulnerability leading to a denial of service (DoS).|insight=The following flaws exist:
- CVE-2024-6232: Regular expressions that allowed excessive backtracking during tarfile.TarFile header parsing are vulnerable to ReDoS via specifically-crafted tar archives.
- CVE-2024-7592: When parsing cookies that contained backslashes for quoted characters in the cookie value, the parser would use an algorithm with quadratic complexity, resulting in excess CPU resources being used while parsing the value.
- CVE-2024-8088: When iterating over names of entries in a zip archive (for example, methods of 'zipfile.Path' like 'namelist()', 'iterdir()', etc) the process can be put into an infinite loop with a maliciously crafted zip archive. This defect applies when reading only metadata or extracting the contents of the zip archive.This vulnerability only affects zipfile.Path, while the more common API zipfile.ZipFile class is unaffected.
- CVE-2024-45490, CVE-2024-45491, CVE-2024-45492: Vulnerabilities in libexpat prior to version 2.6.3|affected=Python prior to version 3.8.20, 3.9.x prior to 3.9.20, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.15, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.10 and 3.12.x prior to 3.12.6.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.8.20, 3.9.20, 3.10.15, 3.11.10, 3.12.6 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 3.11.2 Fixed version: 3.11.10 Installation path / port: unknown

result.name: Python Multiple Vulnerabilities (Jun 2025) - Windows

nvt.name: Python Multiple Vulnerabilities (Jun 2025) - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:P|summary=Python is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following flaws exist:
- CVE-2024-12718: Allows modifying some file metadata (e.g. last modified) with filter='data' or file permissions (chmod) with filter='tar' of files outside the extraction directory.
- CVE-2025-4138: Allows creating arbitrary symlinks outside the extraction directory during extraction with filter='data'.
- CVE-2025-4330: Allows the extraction filter to be ignored, allowing symlink targets to point outside the destination directory, and the modification of some file metadata.
- CVE-2025-4435: Filtered members not skipped with TarFile.errorlevel = 0
- CVE-2025-4517: Allows arbitrary filesystem writes outside the extraction directory during extraction with filter='data'.|affected=Python versions prior to 3.9.23, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.18, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.13, 3.12.x prior to 3.12.11 and 3.13.x prior to 3.13.4.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.9.23, 3.10.18, 3.11.13, 3.12.11, 3.13.4 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 3.11.2 Fixed version: 3.11.13 Installation path / port: unknown

result.name: Ghostscript Arbitrary Code Execution Vulnerability

nvt.name: Ghostscript Arbitrary Code Execution Vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=Ghostscript is prone to an arbitrary code execution vulnerability.|insight=The flaw is due to Off-by-one error in the TrueType bytecode interpreter in Ghostscript that allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (heap memory corruption) via a malformed TrueType font in a document.|affected=Ghostscript versions prior to 8.71|impact=Successful exploitation allows the attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service.|solution=Upgrade to Ghostscript version 8.71 or later.|vuldetect=|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Upgrade to Ghostscript version 8.71 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 8.64 Fixed version: 8.71 Installation path / port: c:\Program Files (x86)\gs\gs8.64\

result.name: Ghostscript 'iscan.c' PDF Handling Remote Buffer Overflow Vulnerability

nvt.name: Ghostscript 'iscan.c' PDF Handling Remote Buffer Overflow Vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=Ghostscript is prone to a buffer overflow vulnerability.|insight=The flaw is due to improper bounds checking by 'iscan.c' when processing malicious 'PDF' files, which leads to open a specially-crafted PDF file.|affected=Ghostscript version 8.64 and prior|impact=Successful exploitation allows the attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted PDF document containing a long name.|solution=Upgrade to Ghostscript version 8.71 or later.|vuldetect=|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Upgrade to Ghostscript version 8.71 or later.

result.name: Ghostscript Multiple Buffer Overflow Vulnerabilities - Windows

nvt.name: Ghostscript Multiple Buffer Overflow Vulnerabilities - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=Ghostscript is prone to a buffer overflow vulnerability.|insight=These flaws arise due to:
- a boundary error in the jbig2_symbol_dict.c() function in the JBIG2 decoding library (jbig2dec) while decoding JBIG2 symbol dictionary segments.
- multiple integer overflows in icc.c in the ICC Format library while processing malformed PDF and PostScript files with embedded images.|affected=Ghostscript version 8.64 and prior on Windows.|impact=Successful exploitation allows the attacker to execute arbitrary code in the context of the affected application and can cause denial of service.|solution=Upgrade to Ghostscript version 8.71 or later.|vuldetect=|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Upgrade to Ghostscript version 8.71 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 8.64 Fixed version: 8.71 Installation path / port: c:\Program Files (x86)\gs\gs8.64\

result.name: Ghostscript Parser Buffer Overflow Vulnerabilities - Windows

nvt.name: Ghostscript Parser Buffer Overflow Vulnerabilities - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=Ghostscript is prone to a buffer overflow vulnerability.|insight=These flaws are due to:
- Boundary error in the 'parser()' which allows the attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted PostScript file.
- Buffer overflow and memory corruption errors when processing a recursive procedure invocations, which could be exploited to crash an affected application or execute arbitrary code.|affected=Ghostscript version 8.70 and 8.64 on Windows.|impact=Successful exploitation allows the attacker to execute arbitrary code in the context of the affected application and can cause denial of service.|solution=No known solution was made available for at least one year since the disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.|vuldetect=|solution_type=WillNotFix
nvt.solution: No known solution was made available for at least one year since the disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.

result.name: Python DoS Vulnerability (Dec 2025) - Windows

nvt.name: Python DoS Vulnerability (Dec 2025) - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:H|summary=Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=When reading an HTTP response from a server, if no read amount is specified, the default behavior will be to use Content-Length. This allows a malicious server to cause the client to read large amounts of data into memory, potentially causing out-of-memory (OOM) or other DoS conditions.|affected=Python prior to version 3.13.11 and 3.14.x prior to 3.14.1.
Note: Fixes have been backported to maintenance branches (3.10, 3.12) but specific patch versions are not yet available.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.14.1 or later, or to the latest maintenance release of your Python version series (3.10.x, 3.12.x) once patches are available. Please see the references for more information.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 3.14.1 or later, or to the latest maintenance release of your Python version series (3.10.x, 3.12.x) once patches are available. Please see the references for more information.

result.name: OpenSSL DoS Vulnerability (20250930, CVE-2025-9230) - Windows

nvt.name: OpenSSL DoS Vulnerability (20250930, CVE-2025-9230) - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:C|summary=OpenSSL is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability due to a out-of-bounds read & write in RFC 3211 KEK Unwrap.|insight=An application trying to decrypt CMS messages encrypted using password based encryption can trigger an out-of-bounds read and write.|affected=OpenSSL version 1.0.2, 1.1.1, 3.0, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4 and 3.5.
Notes:
- The FIPS modules in 3.5, 3.4, 3.3, 3.2, 3.1 and 3.0 are not affected by this issue, as the CMS implementation is outside the OpenSSL FIPS module boundary
- The EOL version 3.1.x is assumed to be affected as well|impact=This out-of-bounds read may trigger a crash which leads to denial of service for an application. The out-of-bounds write can cause a memory corruption which can have various consequences including a denial of service or execution of attacker-supplied code.|solution=Update to version 1.0.2zm, 1.1.1zd, 3.0.18, 3.2.6, 3.3.5, 3.4.3, 3.5.4 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 3.1.3 Fixed version: 3.2.6 Installation path / port: C:\xampp\php\extras\openssl\

result.name: Windows IExpress Untrusted Search Path Vulnerability

nvt.name: Windows IExpress Untrusted Search Path Vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=This host has IExpress bundled with Microsoft Windows and is prone to an untrusted search path vulnerability.|insight=The flaw exists due to an untrusted search path error in self-extracting archive files created by IExpress bundled with Microsoft Windows.|affected=IExpress bundled with Microsoft Windows|impact=Successful exploitation will allow an attacker to execute arbitrary code with the privilege of the user invoking a vulnerable self-extracting archive file.|solution=As a workaround save self-extracting archive files into a newly created directory, and confirm there are no unrelated files in the directory and make sure there are no suspicious files in the directory where self-extracting archive files are saved.|vuldetect=Check for the presence of IExpress (IEXPRESS.EXE).|solution_type=Workaround
nvt.solution: As a workaround save self-extracting archive files into a newly created directory, and confirm there are no unrelated files in the directory and make sure there are no suspicious files in the directory where self-extracting archive files are saved.

result.description: Fixed version: Workaround File checked: C:\Windows\system32\IEXPRESS.EXE File version: 11.0.22621.1

nvt.name: OpenSSL DoS Vulnerability (20250930, CVE-2025-9230) - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:C|summary=OpenSSL is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability due to a out-of-bounds read & write in RFC 3211 KEK Unwrap.|insight=An application trying to decrypt CMS messages encrypted using password based encryption can trigger an out-of-bounds read and write.|affected=OpenSSL version 1.0.2, 1.1.1, 3.0, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4 and 3.5.
Notes:
- The FIPS modules in 3.5, 3.4, 3.3, 3.2, 3.1 and 3.0 are not affected by this issue, as the CMS implementation is outside the OpenSSL FIPS module boundary
- The EOL version 3.1.x is assumed to be affected as well|impact=This out-of-bounds read may trigger a crash which leads to denial of service for an application. The out-of-bounds write can cause a memory corruption which can have various consequences including a denial of service or execution of attacker-supplied code.|solution=Update to version 1.0.2zm, 1.1.1zd, 3.0.18, 3.2.6, 3.3.5, 3.4.3, 3.5.4 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Python Command Injection Vulnerability (Oct 2024) - Windows

nvt.name: Python Command Injection Vulnerability (Oct 2024) - Windows
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Python is prone to a command injection vulnerability in the venv module.|insight=A vulnerability has been found in the venv module and CLI where path names provided when creating a virtual environment were not quoted properly, allowing the virtual environment creator to inject commands into virtual environment 'activation' scripts (ie 'source venv/bin/activate'). This means that attacker-controlled virtual environments are able to run commands when the virtual environment is activated. Virtual environments which are not created by an attacker or which aren't activated before being used (ie './venv/bin/python') are not affected.|affected=Python versions prior to 3.9.21, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.16, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.11, 3.12.x prior to 3.12.8 and 3.13.x prior to 3.13.1.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.9.21, 3.10.16, 3.11.11, 3.12.8, 3.13.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability in libexpat.|insight=libexpat allows a denial of service (resource consumption) because many full reparsings are required in the case of a large token for which multiple buffer fills are needed.
Also, on Windows urllib.request no longer resolves the hostname before checking it against the system's proxy bypass list.|affected=Python prior to version 3.8.19, 3.9.x prior to 3.9.19, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.14, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.9 and 3.12.x prior to 3.12.3.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.8.19, 3.9.19, 3.10.14, 3.11.9, 3.12.3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: OpenSSL Incorrect Cipher Key & IV Length Processing Vulnerability (20231024) - Windows

nvt.name: OpenSSL Incorrect Cipher Key & IV Length Processing Vulnerability (20231024) - Windows
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N|summary=OpenSSL is prone to an incorrect processing of key and initialisation vector (IV) lengths vulnerability.|insight=When calling EVP_EncryptInit_ex2(), EVP_DecryptInit_ex2() or EVP_CipherInit_ex2() the provided OSSL_PARAM array is processed after the key and IV have been established. Any alterations to the key length, via the 'keylen' parameter or the IV length, via the 'ivlen' parameter, within the OSSL_PARAM array will not take effect as intended, potentially causing truncation or overreading of these values. The following ciphers and cipher modes are impacted: RC2, RC4, RC5, CCM, GCM and OCB.|affected=OpenSSL version 3.0 and 3.1.|impact=A truncation in the IV can result in non-uniqueness, which could result in loss of confidentiality for some cipher modes.|solution=Update to version 3.0.12, 3.1.4 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 3.1.3 Fixed version: 3.1.4 Installation path / port: C:\xampp\php\extras\openssl\

result.name: Python <= 3.12.1 'RecursionError' Vulnerability - Windows

nvt.name: Python <= 3.12.1 'RecursionError' Vulnerability - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Python is prone to a 'RecursionError' vulnerability.|insight=The legacy email.utils.parseaddr function allows attackers to trigger 'RecursionError: maximum recursion depth exceeded while calling a Python object' via a crafted argument. This argument is plausibly an untrusted value from an application's input data that was supposed to contain a name and an e-mail address.
NOTE: The vendor's perspective is that this is neither a vulnerability nor a bug. The email package is intended to have size limits and to throw an exception when limits are exceeded.|affected=The legacy email.utils.parseaddr function in Python through 3.12.1.|impact=|solution=No known solution was made available for at least one year since the disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.
NOTE: email.utils.parseaddr is categorized as a Legacy API in the documentation of the Python email package. Applications should instead use the email.parser.BytesParser or email.parser.Parser class.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=WillNotFix
nvt.solution:
No known solution was made available for at least one year since the disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.
NOTE: email.utils.parseaddr is categorized as a Legacy API in the documentation of the Python email package. Applications should instead use the email.parser.BytesParser or email.parser.Parser class.

result.description: Installed version: 3.11.2 Fixed version: None Installation path / port: unknown

result.name: Microsoft Windows HID Functionality (Over USB) Code Execution Vulnerability (Jan 2011)

nvt.name: Microsoft Windows HID Functionality (Over USB) Code Execution Vulnerability (Jan 2011)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:L/AC:M/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=A USB device driver software is prone to a code execution vulnerability.|insight=The flaw is due to error in USB device driver (hidserv.dll), which does not properly warn the user before enabling additional Human Interface Device (HID) functionality.|affected=All Microsoft Windows systems with an enabled USB device driver and no local protection mechanism against the automatic enabling of additional Human Interface Device (HID).|impact=Successful exploitation will allow user-assisted attackers to execute arbitrary programs via crafted USB data.|solution=No solution or patch was made available for at least one year since disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.
A workaround is to introduce device filtering on the target host to only allow trusted USB devices to be enabled automatically. Once this workaround is in place an overwrite for this vulnerability can be created to mark it as a false positive.|vuldetect=Checks via SMB if a specific device driver (hidserv.dll) exists on the target system.|solution_type=Workaround
nvt.solution:
No solution or patch was made available for at least one year since disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.
A workaround is to introduce device filtering on the target host to only allow trusted USB devices to be enabled automatically. Once this workaround is in place an overwrite for this vulnerability can be created to mark it as a false positive.

result.description: File checked for existence: C:\Windows\system32\hidserv.dll

result.name: Microsoft Windows HID Functionality (Over USB) Code Execution Vulnerability (Jan 2011)

nvt.name: Microsoft Windows HID Functionality (Over USB) Code Execution Vulnerability (Jan 2011)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:L/AC:M/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=A USB device driver software is prone to a code execution vulnerability.|insight=The flaw is due to error in USB device driver (hidserv.dll), which does not properly warn the user before enabling additional Human Interface Device (HID) functionality.|affected=All Microsoft Windows systems with an enabled USB device driver and no local protection mechanism against the automatic enabling of additional Human Interface Device (HID).|impact=Successful exploitation will allow user-assisted attackers to execute arbitrary programs via crafted USB data.|solution=No solution or patch was made available for at least one year since disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.
A workaround is to introduce device filtering on the target host to only allow trusted USB devices to be enabled automatically. Once this workaround is in place an overwrite for this vulnerability can be created to mark it as a false positive.|vuldetect=Checks via SMB if a specific device driver (hidserv.dll) exists on the target system.|solution_type=Workaround
nvt.solution:
No solution or patch was made available for at least one year since disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or replace the product by another one.
A workaround is to introduce device filtering on the target host to only allow trusted USB devices to be enabled automatically. Once this workaround is in place an overwrite for this vulnerability can be created to mark it as a false positive.

result.description: File checked for existence: C:\Windows\system32\hidserv.dll

nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:S/C:P/I:P/A:N|summary=Python is prone to an access control vulnerability.|insight=On Windows a directory returned by tempfile.mkdtemp() would not always have permissions set to restrict reading and writing to the temporary directory by other users, instead usually inheriting the correct permissions from the default location. result.name: Python Connection Race Vulnerability (Jul 2024) - Windows

nvt.name: Python Connection Race Vulnerability (Jul 2024) - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:S/C:P/I:P/A:N|summary=Python is prone to a connection race vulnerability.|insight=The 'socket' module provides a pure-Python fallback to the socket.socketpair() function for platforms that don't support AF_UNIX, such as Windows. This pure-Python implementation uses AF_INET or AF_INET6 to create a local connected pair of sockets. The connection between the two sockets was not verified before passing the two sockets back to the user, which leaves the server socket vulnerable to a connection race from a malicious local peer.|affected=Python version 3.5 prior to version 3.8.20, 3.9.x prior to 3.9.20, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.15, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.10 and 3.12.x prior to 3.12.5.
Platforms that support AF_UNIX such as Linux and macOS are not affected by this vulnerability.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.8.20, 3.9.20, 3.10.15, 3.11.10, 3.12.5 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 3.11.2 Fixed version: 3.11.10 Installation path / port: unknown

result.name: Python Untrusted Search Path Vulnerability (Feb 2025) - Windows

nvt.name: Python Untrusted Search Path Vulnerability (Feb 2025) - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:S/C:N/I:P/A:P|summary=Python is prone to an untrusted search path vulnerability in the mimetypes standard library.|insight=There is a defect in the CPython standard library module 'mimetypes' where on Windows the default list of known file locations are writable meaning other users can create invalid files to cause MemoryError to be raised on Python runtime startup or have file extensions be interpreted as the incorrect file type.
This defect is caused by the default locations of Linux and macOS platforms (such as '/etc/mime.types') also being used on Windows, where they are user-writable locations ('C:\etc\mime.types').|affected=Python versions prior to 3.14.0.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.14.0 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 3.11.2 Fixed version: 3.14.0 Installation path / port: unknown

result.name: Python 'zipfile' Module Vulnerability (Oct 2025) - Windows

nvt.name: Python 'zipfile' Module Vulnerability (Oct 2025) - Windows
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:P/A:N|summary=Python is prone to a vulnerability in the 'zipfile' module.|insight=The 'zipfile' module would not check the validity of the ZIP64 End of Central Directory (EOCD) Locator record offset value would not be used to locate the ZIP64 EOCD record, instead the ZIP64 EOCD record would be assumed to be the previous record in the ZIP archive. This could be abused to create ZIP archives that are handled differently by the 'zipfile' module compared to other ZIP implementations. Remediation maintains this behavior, but checks that the offset specified in the ZIP64 EOCD Locator record matches the expected value.|affected=Python prior to version 3.9.24, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.19, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.14, 3.12.x prior to 3.12.12, 3.13.x prior to 3.13.10 and 3.14.x prior to 3.14.1.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.9.24, 3.10.19, 3.11.14, 3.12.12, 3.13.10, 3.14.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Python IP Ranges Vulnerability (Jun 2024) - Windows

nvt.name: Python IP Ranges Vulnerability (Jun 2024) - Windows
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=Python is prone to a vulnerability in the ipaddress module.|insight=The 'ipaddress' module contained incorrect information about whether certain IPv4 and IPv6 addresses were designated as 'globally reachable' or 'private'. This affected the 'is_private' and 'is_global' properties of the ipaddress.IPv4Address, ipaddress.IPv4Network, ipaddress.IPv6Address, and ipaddress.IPv6Network classes, where values wouldn't be returned in accordance with the latest information from the IANA Special-Purpose Address Registries.|affected=Python prior to version 3.8.20, 3.9.x prior to 3.9.20, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.15, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.10 and 3.12.x prior to 3.12.4.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.8.20, 3.9.20, 3.10.15, 3.11.10, 3.12.4 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: OpenSSL OOB Memory Access Vulnerability (20241016) - Windows

nvt.name: OpenSSL OOB Memory Access Vulnerability (20241016) - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:P|summary=OpenSSL is prone to an out of bound (OOB) memory access vulnerability.|insight=Use of the low-level GF(2^m) elliptic curve APIs with untrusted explicit values for the field polynomial can lead to out-of-bounds memory reads or writes.|affected=OpenSSL versions 1.0.2, 1.1.1, 3.0, 3.1, 3.2 and 3.3.|impact=Out of bound memory writes can lead to an application crash or even a possibility of a remote code execution, however, in all the protocols involving Elliptic Curve Cryptography that we're aware of, either only 'named curves' are supported, or, if explicit curve parameters are supported, they specify an X9.62 encoding of binary (GF(2^m)) curves that can't represent problematic input values. Thus the likelihood of existence of a vulnerable application is low.|solution=Update to version 1.0.2zl, 1.1.1zb, 3.0.16, 3.1.8, 3.2.4, 3.3.3 or later once available.
Note: As of 01/2025 these updates have not been released yet.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution:
Update to version 1.0.2zl, 1.1.1zb, 3.0.16, 3.1.8, 3.2.4, 3.3.3 or later once available.
Note: As of 01/2025 these updates have not been released yet.

result.description: Installed version: 3.1.3 Fixed version: 3.1.8 Installation path / port: C:\xampp\apache\bin\

result.name: OpenSSL Timing Side-Channel Vulnerability (20250120) - Windows

nvt.name: OpenSSL Timing Side-Channel Vulnerability (20250120) - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=OpenSSL is prone to a timing side-channel vulnerability.|insight=A timing side-channel which could potentially allow recovering the private key exists in the ECDSA signature computation.|affected=OpenSSL versions 1.0.2, 1.1.1, 3.0, 3.1, 3.2, 3.3 and 3.4.|impact=A timing side-channel in ECDSA signature computations could allow recovering the private key by an attacker. However, measuring the timing would require either local access to the signing application or a very fast network connection with low latency.
There is a timing signal of around 300 nanoseconds when the top word of the inverted ECDSA nonce value is zero. This can happen with significant probability only for some of the supported elliptic curves. In particular the NIST P-521 curve is affected. To be able to measure this leak, the attacker process must either be located in the same physical computer or must have a very fast network connection with low latency. For that reason the severity of this vulnerability is Low.|solution=Update to version 1.0.2zl, 1.1.1zb, 3.0.16, 3.1.8, 3.2.4, 3.3.3, 3.4.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 3.1.3 Fixed version: 3.1.8 Installation path / port: C:\xampp\php\extras\openssl\

However, measuring the timing would require either local access to the signing application or a very fast network connection with low latency.
There is a timing signal of around 300 nanoseconds when the top word of the inverted ECDSA nonce value is zero. This can happen with significant probability only for some of the supported elliptic curves. In particular the NIST P-521 curve is affected. To be able to measure this leak, the attacker process must either be located in the same physical computer or must have a very fast network connection with low latency. For that reason the severity of this vulnerability is Low.|solution=Update to version 1.0.2zl, 1.1.1zb, 3.0.16, 3.1.8, 3.2.4, 3.3.3, 3.4.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: OpenSSL OOB Memory Access Vulnerability (20241016) - Windows

nvt.name: OpenSSL OOB Memory Access Vulnerability (20241016) - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:P|summary=OpenSSL is prone to an out of bound (OOB) memory access vulnerability.|insight=Use of the low-level GF(2^m) elliptic curve APIs with untrusted explicit values for the field polynomial can lead to out-of-bounds memory reads or writes.|affected=OpenSSL versions 1.0.2, 1.1.1, 3.0, 3.1, 3.2 and 3.3.|impact=Out of bound memory writes can lead to an application crash or even a possibility of a remote code execution, however, in all the protocols involving Elliptic Curve Cryptography that we're aware of, either only 'named curves' are supported, or, if explicit curve parameters are supported, they specify an X9.62 encoding of binary (GF(2^m)) curves that can't represent problematic input values. Thus the likelihood of existence of a vulnerable application is low.|solution=Update to version 1.0.2zl, 1.1.1zb, 3.0.16, 3.1.8, 3.2.4, 3.3.3 or later once available.
Note: As of 01/2025 these updates have not been released yet.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution:
Update to version 1.0.2zl, 1.1.1zb, 3.0.16, 3.1.8, 3.2.4, 3.3.3 or later once available.
Note: As of 01/2025 these updates have not been released yet.

nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:P|summary=OpenSSL is prone to a buffer overread vulnerability.|insight=Calling the OpenSSL API function SSL_select_next_proto with an empty supported client protocols buffer may cause a crash or memory contents to be sent to the peer.|affected=OpenSSL versions 1.0.2, 1.1.1, 3.0, 3.1, 3.2 and 3.3.|impact=A buffer overread can have a range of potential consequences such as unexpected application behaviour or a crash.|solution=Update to version 1.0.2zk, 1.1.1za, 3.0.15, 3.1.7, 3.2.3, 3.3.2 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Apache Log4j 2.x < 2.25.3 Missing TLS Hostname Verification Vulnerability - Windows

nvt.name: Apache Log4j 2.x < 2.25.3 Missing TLS Hostname Verification Vulnerability - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:L/A:N|summary=Apache Log4j is prone to a missing TLS hostname verification vulnerability in the socket appender.|insight=The Socket Appender does not perform TLS hostname verification of the peer certificate, even when the verifyHostName configuration attribute or the log4j2.sslVerifyHostName system property is set to true.|affected=Apache Log4j versions 2.0-beta9 through 2.25.2.|impact=This issue may allow a man-in-the-middle attacker to intercept or redirect log traffic under the following conditions:
- The attacker is able to intercept or redirect network traffic between the client and the log receiver.
- The attacker can present a server certificate issued by a certification authority trusted by the Socket Appender's configured trust store (or by the default Java trust store if no custom trust store is configured).|solution=Update to version 2.25.3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: SSL/TLS: Deprecated TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1 Protocol Detection

nvt.name: SSL/TLS: Deprecated TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1 Protocol Detection
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:N/I:P/A:N|summary=It was possible to detect the usage of the deprecated TLSv1.0 and/or TLSv1.1 protocol on this system.|insight=The TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1 protocols contain known cryptographic flaws like:
- CVE-2011-3389: Browser Exploit Against SSL/TLS (BEAST)
- CVE-2015-0204: Factoring Attack on RSA-EXPORT Keys Padding Oracle On Downgraded Legacy Encryption (FREAK)|affected=- All services providing an encrypted communication using the TLSv1.0 and/or TLSv1.1 protocols
- CVE-2023-41928: Kiloview P1 4G and P2 4G Video Encoder
- CVE-2024-41270: Gorush v1.18.4
- CVE-2025-3200: Multiple products from Wiesemann & Theis|impact=An attacker might be able to use the known cryptographic flaws to eavesdrop the connection between clients and the service to get access to sensitive data transferred within the secured connection.
Furthermore newly uncovered vulnerabilities in this protocols won't receive security updates anymore.|solution=It is recommended to disable the deprecated TLSv1.0 and/or TLSv1.1 protocols in favor of the TLSv1.2+ protocols.
Please see the references for more resources supporting you with this task.|vuldetect=Checks the used TLS protocols of the services provided by this system.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
It is recommended to disable the deprecated TLSv1.0 and/or TLSv1.1 protocols in favor of the TLSv1.2+ protocols.
Please see the references for more resources supporting you with this task.

result.description: In addition to TLSv1.2+ the service is also providing the deprecated TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1 protocols and supports one or more ciphers. Those supported ciphers can be found in the 'SSL/TLS: Report Supported Cipher Suites' (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.802067) VT.

result.name: Python Use After Free Vulnerability (May 2025) - Windows

nvt.name: Python Use After Free Vulnerability (May 2025) - Windows
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:L/AC:H/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:C|summary=Python is prone to an use after free vulnerability.|insight=There is an issue in CPython when using bytes.decode(). If you are not using the 'unicode_escape' encoding or an error handler your usage is not affected. To work-around this issue you may stop using the error= handler and instead wrap the bytes.decode() call in a try-except catching the DecodeError.|affected=Python prior to version 3.9.23, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.18, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.13, 3.12.x prior to 3.12.11 and 3.13.x prior to 3.13.4.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.9.23, 3.10.18, 3.11.13, 3.12.11, 3.13.4 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Python SSRF Vulnerability (Nov 2024) - Windows

nvt.name: Python SSRF Vulnerability (Nov 2024) - Windows
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:N/I:P/A:N|summary=Python is prone to a server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerability.|insight=The urllib.parse.urlsplit() and urlparse() functions improperly validated bracketed hosts ([]), allowing hosts that weren't IPv6 or IPvFuture. This behavior was not conformant to RFC 3986 and potentially enabled SSRF if a URL is processed by more than one URL parser.|affected=Python versions prior to 3.9.21, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.16 and 3.11.x prior to 3.11.4.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.9.21, 3.10.16, 3.11.4 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Python Improper Input Validation Vulnerability (Jan 2025) - Windows

nvt.name: Python Improper Input Validation Vulnerability (Jan 2025) - Windows
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:N/I:P/A:N|summary=Python is prone to an improper input validation vulnerability in the urllib.parse.urlsplit and urlparse standard functions.|insight=The Python standard library functions urllib.parse.urlsplit and urlparse accepted domain names that included square brackets which isn't valid according to RFC 3986. Square brackets are only meant to be used as delimiters for specifying IPv6 and IPvFuture hosts in URLs. This could result in differential parsing across the Python URL parser and other specification-compliant URL parsers.|affected=Python versions prior to 3.9.22, 3.10.x prior to 3.10.17, 3.11.x prior to 3.11.12, 3.12.x prior to 3.12.9 and 3.13.x prior to 3.13.2.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.9.22, 3.10.17, 3.11.12, 3.12.9, 3.13.2 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 3.11.2 Fixed version: 3.11.12 Installation path / port: unknown

result.name: Node.js < 20.19.2, 22.x < 22.15.1 DoS Vulnerability - Windows

nvt.name: Node.js < 20.19.2, 22.x < 22.15.1 DoS Vulnerability - Windows
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:P|summary=Node.js is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=In Node.js, the `ReadFileUtf8` internal binding leaks memory due to a corrupted pointer in `uv_fs_s.file`: a UTF-16 path buffer is allocated but subsequently overwritten when the file descriptor is set. This results in an unrecoverable memory leak on every call. Repeated use can cause unbounded memory growth, leading to a denial of service.|affected=Node.js prior to version 20.19.2, 21.x prior to 22.15.1.|impact=|solution=Update to version 20.19.2, 22.15.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: Python Improper Encoding of Output Vulnerability (Feb 2025) - Windows

nvt.name: Python Improper Encoding of Output Vulnerability (Feb 2025) - Windows
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:H/Au:S/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=Python is prone to an improper encoding of output vulnerability.|insight=During an address list folding when a separating comma ends up on a folded line and that line is to be unicode-encoded then the separator itself is also unicode-encoded. Expected behavior is that the separating comma remains a plan comma. This can result in the address header being misinterpreted by some mail servers.|affected=Python versions prior to 3.11.10 and 3.12.x prior to 3.12.4.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.11.10, 3.12.4 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure

nvt.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:L/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.|insight=The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.|affected=|impact=This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.|solution=Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)|vuldetect=Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)

result.description: The following response / ICMP packet has been received: - ICMP Type: 14 - ICMP Code: 0

result.name: Python DoS Vulnerability (Dec 2025) - Windows

nvt.name: Python DoS Vulnerability (Dec 2025) - Windows
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:L/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:P|summary=Python is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=When loading a plist file, the plistlib module reads data in size specified by the file itself, meaning a malicious file can cause out-of-memory (OOM) and DoS issues.|affected=Python prior to version 3.13.10 and 3.14.x prior to 3.14.1.
Note: Fixes have been backported to maintenance branches (3.10, 3.12) but specific patch versions are not yet available.|impact=|solution=Update to version 3.13.10, 3.14.1 or later, or to the latest maintenance release of your Python version series (3.10.x, 3.12.x) once patches are available. Please see the references for more information.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 3.13.10, 3.14.1 or later, or to the latest maintenance release of your Python version series (3.10.x, 3.12.x) once patches are available. Please see the references for more information.
